D10 Imaging L5 Evaluate Reference

Foveon — Silicon Absorption Depth Separates Colour

no colour filter array — short-wavelength light is absorbed near the surface, long-wavelength light penetrates deeper; three stacked photodiodes read each layer

The Foveon X3 sensor exploits a physical fact: silicon's absorption coefficient α(λ) depends on wavelength. Blue (~450 nm) is absorbed within ~0.2–0.3 μm of the surface; green within ~1 μm; red penetrates ~3 μm before absorption. Stacking three photodiodes at those depths reads each band without a colour filter array — every pixel measures all three.

Drag the incident wavelength. The cross-section shows the intensity profile down the silicon; the three photodiode markers register their per-layer signal. Compare with the Bayer alternative — Foveon has no demosaic but suffers more dark noise per channel because each photodiode is small.
